Full job description
Operations Manager – Park Operations

Peak Wildlife Park, Staffordshire

About the Role

We're looking for an experienced Operations Manager to take full ownership of the day-to-day running of Peak Wildlife Park. This is a visible, hands-on leadership role. You'll be on the ground, managing people, solving problems, and making sure every part of the park is running to the highest standard.

This is a genuine opportunity to shape how a growing wildlife attraction operates, with a clear path for the right person to progress into a senior leadership position.

What You'll Be Doing

Daily Operations

Take full ownership of the park's day-to-day operation. Ensure all areas are open, staffed, and performing to a consistently high standard. Act as the senior operational lead on site.

Team & Staffing Management

Directly manage a front-of-house team of around 40 staff across catering and guest services. You'll work with and develop Operations Supervisors, helping to build a strong team structure as the park grows. You'll work closely alongside the Head Keeper, as the animal and front-of-house teams operate as equals, and strong collaboration between the two is key to how the park runs.

Guest Experience & Standards

Maintain high standards across every area of the park. Ensure visitors have a safe, enjoyable, and well-managed experience. Ensure any issues are dealt with swiftly and to a high standard.

Planning & Delivery

Plan ahead for busy periods, seasonal peaks, and events, ensuring the park is always operationally ready and nothing is left to chance.

Reporting & Communication

Provide clear daily and weekly updates to senior management. Flag risks early, communicate across teams effectively, and drive continuous improvement.

What We're Looking For

Essential:

- Proven experience in operations management within a fast-paced environment (visitor attraction, leisure, hospitality, retail, or similar)

- Demonstrated ability to lead and manage large teams

- Experience handling peak trading periods and operational pressure

- A practical, solution-focused mindset with high personal standards

You'll thrive here if you:

- Take ownership without being told to

- Stay calm and make good decisions under pressure

- Hold yourself and your team to a high standard

- Communicate clearly and directly

This role isn't for you if you prefer to stay behind a desk, avoid difficult decisions, or need constant direction.

Working Pattern

This role includes weekends, bank holidays, and peak trading periods. If you thrive in a busy, varied environment, you'll enjoy the pace here.

Salary & Benefits

- £40,000 – £50,000 depending on experience

- The opportunity to take on real ownership and progress. We're actively looking to develop this role into a senior leadership position as the park grows.

About Peak Wildlife Park

Peak Wildlife Park is a growing visitor attraction with real ambition. This is a great time to join. If you want a role where your impact is real and your progression is possible, we'd love to hear from you.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

personalisedContent.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to apply for the Operations Manager position at Peak Wildlife Park.

I have spent my career in fast-moving operational roles where standards, people and delivery all have to hold together under pressure. My background includes leisure operations at Merlin Entertainments, operations leadership in specialist manufacturing, and my current role in NHS primary care, where I lead performance, service control and improvement across a live service environment.

What interests me about Peak Wildlife Park is the chance to take real ownership of a growing visitor attraction and help shape how it operates as it develops. The role calls for someone who is hands-on, calm under pressure and clear with people, and that is the kind of environment I work best in. I am used to being close to the operation, making practical decisions, and keeping teams focused on what matters most.

I would bring experience of leading busy operations, improving guest and customer experience, and keeping performance visible through clear reporting and control. At Alton Towers Resort I worked in live leisure operations across major events and seasonal peaks, and in my current role I have improved patient experience, strengthened reporting and supported financial improvement. I have also led day-to-day operations through a scale-up phase in manufacturing, where resilience and discipline were essential.

I would welcome the opportunity to bring that experience to Peak Wildlife Park and help support the park’s growth with strong day-to-day control, clear communication and high standards.

Yours faithfully,

Ben Howard
contentNotes: I used three main evidence strands from leisure, healthcare and manufacturing to show fit without overloading the page.
Company-specific facts were kept limited to the advert’s description of growth, ambition and progression.
I avoided repeating the role title or sector across multiple fields to keep the page more natural and selective.
